title: Overlay buy/sell signal arrows/labels directly on the candlestick chart

requirements.summary: Overlay buy/sell signal markers directly on the candlestick chart canvas with pattern names and confidence scores
acceptanceCriteria: A green upward arrow appears below the low of any candle that produced a BUY signal.
A red downward arrow appears above the high of any candle that produced a SELL signal.
Each signal marker displays the dominant pattern name and confidence score as a small text label.
Signal markers pan and zoom in sync with the candlestick chart (they are part of the canvas draw cycle).
No signal marker is rendered for NEUTRAL or TBC signals that did not reach a clear BUY/SELL threshold.
Markers do not obscure price data and are visually distinct from EMA lines and support/resistance bands.

file_operations.description: Add a new SignalMarker interface to represent a buy/sell signal marker with position, type (BUY/SELL), pattern name, confidence score, and candle index. This type will be used to pass signal data to the CandlestickChart component for rendering.

Modify the aggregateFinalSignal function to return an array of SignalMarker objects alongside the FinalSignal. For each candle in the historical data, if the aggregated signal is BUY or SELL (not NEUTRAL or TBC-only), create a SignalMarker entry with the candle's index, low/high price, signal type, dominant pattern name, and confidence score. Store these markers for consumption by the chart component.

Update the App component to collect SignalMarker data during the technical analysis pass. Pass the array of signal markers as a new prop (signalMarkers) to the CandlestickChart component. Ensure signal markers are regenerated only when candles change or a new technical analysis run occurs, to avoid unnecessary recomputation.

Add a signalMarkers prop (SignalMarker[]) to the CandlestickChart component. In the canvas draw cycle, after rendering candles and overlays, iterate through the signalMarkers array and render each marker: for BUY signals, draw a green upward-pointing triangle/arrow below the candle's low wick; for SELL signals, draw a red downward-pointing triangle/arrow above the candle's high wick. Each arrow should be followed by a small text label showing the pattern name and confidence percentage (e.g., 'Hammer 78%'). Use the existing price-to-canvas coordinate transformation so markers pan and zoom correctly. Ensure arrow size and label font are legible but not obtrusive, and use a slight background or stroke to keep labels readable over busy chart areas.
